content: SSE vs WebSocket for server-to-many-clients fan-out: SSE wins for unidirectional push scenarios. SSE runs over HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2, auto-reconnects via EventSource, costs ~2-5 KiB server-side state vs ~50 KiB for WebSocket (frame buffers, masking, ping/pong). SSE passes through CDN/proxies without special config. EventSource auto-reconnects with Last-Event-ID. Hono has first-class streamSSE() helper. Browser EventSource is native. WebSocket is needed only for bidirectional (client-to-server messaging beyond initial handshake). For market data fan-out (server pushes to browser, browser only subscribes): SSE is the right choice at the server-to-browser hop. For sidecar-to-TS-server hop: either SSE or WebSocket works; SSE keeps the sidecar simpler (HTTP GET endpoint, no upgrade negotiation).
